Dashboard Archive: 2026-07-09 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's data shows two exceptionally strong momentum performers at the top of the rankings. LABU (3x Leveraged Nasdaq-100) leads with a composite score of 97.2, driven by extraordinary 1-year returns of 437.48% and flawless technical positioning across all four major moving averages. SYRE follows with even more extreme 1-year returns of 532.61% and perfect ratio consistency (5 of 5 passes), though its bad_spy_score of 1.35 is significantly lower than LABU's 4.33, suggesting SYRE may be more vulnerable on market downturns. Both tickers show strong outperformance versus benchmarks and share similar bullish correlation patterns anchored by HYG (high-yield bonds), indicating risk appetite is driving these moves. The data captures a market moment where leveraged tech and speculative equity names are sustaining exceptional momentum with clean technical setups, though the disparity in resilience metrics between these top two suggests varying underlying stability.
